title: Building psychology quoted from wikipedia
wikitext:
[]: Building psychology? (quoted from wikipedia)             Quote Post by hwdge » Tue Mar 12, 2013 7:13 pm "The scenery and buildings in Tokyo-3 often seem laden with psychological import, even in the first episode.[31]" LoLwut am I missing something, I knew the buildings always looked kind of off and weird but having psychological import? I require assistance from those of us more versed with eva psychology.

[]:             Quote Post by lastcody » Tue Mar 12, 2013 7:21 pm For future reference when you say stuff like "LoL wut" its hard to talk to you seriously. Just my opinion though.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Tue Mar 12, 2013 10:53 pm Could you give us a link to the page you read that from? I think that would be a good start for the thread.

[]:             Quote Post by K40s » Wed Mar 13, 2013 12:59 am It's from wikipedia Themes [edit]Psychoanalysis For more details on this topic, see Psychoanalysis. Evangelion has long been taken as a deeply personal expression of Hideaki Anno's personal struggles.[28] From the start, Evangelion invokes many psychological themes. Phrases used in episodes, their titles, and the names of the background music frequently derive from Sigmund Freud's works,[29] in addition to perhaps some Lacanian influences in general.[30] Examples include "Thanatos", "Oral stage", "Separation Anxiety", and "Mother Is the First Other" (the mother as the first object of a child's love is the basis of the Oedipus complex). The scenery and buildings in Tokyo-3 often seem laden with psychological import, even in the first episode.[31]

[]:             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Wed Mar 13, 2013 3:58 am Seems like a pretty basic reading of NGE's setting to me. Consider how, for example, at the end of the series that much of Tokyo III is destroyed and also emptied of many of its citizens (Such as Touji, Kensuke etc)...while Shinji is also mentally damaged and socially isolated himself. Initially coming to Tokyo III was a chance for Shinji to improve his life, but as he completely falls apart, the city that once represented hope and opportunity to him does as well.
